name: Platform Shell and Styles
rationale: This slice establishes the browser entry point, Angular root composition, header shell, global styling, build scripts, and production service-worker hook. Other feature slices are mounted through this shell, so Stage 2/3 can use it as the baseline for routing, layout, and frontend build behavior.

name: Firebase Platform and Security Rules
rationale: Auth and task services both rely on AngularFire providers and the Firebase namespace. The Firebase database rules also define the AS-IS ownership boundary for task data, which downstream local backend slices must preserve.

name: Authentication and Route Access
rationale: This slice owns AS-IS authentication state, sign-in provider flows, sign-out, and guard redirects. It must precede task migration because task persistence is scoped by the authenticated Firebase uid and the `/tasks` route is guarded.

name: Task Data Model and Service
rationale: This slice contains the AS-IS task schema, Firebase timestamp behavior, user-scoped database path, completed filtering query, and create/update/delete methods. It depends on auth for `uid$` and on Firebase for list persistence.

name: Task UI Workflow
rationale: This slice owns the user-visible task workflow: protected `/tasks` page, filter links, task creation form, task list, edit mode, completion toggle, deletion, and focus behavior. It depends on auth guards for route access and the task service for persistence.

name: Test and Quality Harness
rationale: This slice captures the AS-IS quality and operational verification surface: Karma/Jasmine unit tests, Protractor smoke test, TSLint config, and CircleCI build/deploy behavior. It is ordered last because its tests reference the shell and task model and should be replaced after functional slices define target behavior.
